workpharm.title: Work Pharm
workpharm.description: This set represents our complete, in-house pharmacy support.

data.fact: Irreversibly inhibits COX-1 and COX-2. Avoid in children (Reye syndrome). Monitor for tinnitus (toxicity sign).
data.alert: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk; avoid with anticoagulants.

data.fact: COX-2 selective NSAID. Lower GI risk but increased cardiovascular risk. Contraindicated in sulfa allergy.
data.alert: Monitor hepatic function with prolonged acetaminophen use.

data.fact: Clinoril is the trade name for Sulindac. Drug class: Analgesic / NSAID.
data.alert: Assess pain level before and after administration.

data.fact: Max 4g/day in adults. Leading cause of acute liver failure. Antidote: N-acetylcysteine (Mucomyst).
data.alert: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk; avoid with anticoagulants.

data.fact: Mobic is the trade name for Meloxicam. Drug class: Analgesic / NSAID.
data.alert: Assess pain level before and after administration.

data.fact: Longer-acting NSAID. Take with food. Monitor for GI bleeding and renal function.
data.alert: Assess pain level before and after administration.

data.fact: Potent NSAID. IV/IM use limited to 5 days max due to GI/renal toxicity risk.
data.alert: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk; avoid with anticoagulants.

data.fact: Max 4g/day in adults. Leading cause of acute liver failure. Antidote: N-acetylcysteine (Mucomyst).
data.alert: Monitor hepatic function with prolonged acetaminophen use.

data.fact: Max 4g/day in adults. Leading cause of acute liver failure. Antidote: N-acetylcysteine (Mucomyst).
data.alert: Monitor for GI bleeding. Avoid in renal impairment.

data.fact: NSAID available oral and topical. Monitor hepatic function. Increased cardiovascular risk with prolonged use.
data.alert: Monitor hepatic function with prolonged acetaminophen use.

data.fact: Broad-spectrum penicillin. Take full course. Causes maculopapular rash in EBV/mono patients.
data.alert: Report rash, hives, or difficulty breathing immediately.

data.fact: Broad-spectrum penicillin. Take full course. Causes maculopapular rash in EBV/mono patients.
data.alert: Watch for signs of C. difficile: watery diarrhea, fever.

data.fact: Avelox is the trade name for Moxifloxacin. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Assess for allergies before first dose. Monitor C&S results.

data.fact: Bactrim is the trade name for Sulfamethoxazole / Trimethoprim. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Monitor renal and hepatic function during therapy.

data.fact: Cayston is the trade name for Aztreonam. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Report rash, hives, or difficulty breathing immediately.

data.fact: Fluoroquinolone. Avoid dairy/antacids (chelation). Risk of tendon rupture, especially in elderly.
data.alert: Monitor renal and hepatic function during therapy.

data.fact: High risk of C. difficile colitis. Monitor for watery/bloody diarrhea. Stop drug if suspected.
data.alert: Complete the full course of therapy. Monitor for superinfection.

data.fact: Macrolide. GI upset common. Prolongs QT interval. Used for penicillin-allergic patients.
data.alert: Monitor renal and hepatic function during therapy.

data.fact: Floxin is the trade name for Ofloxacin. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Watch for signs of C. difficile: watery diarrhea, fever.

data.fact: Fortaz is the trade name for Ceftazidime. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Watch for signs of C. difficile: watery diarrhea, fever.

data.fact: Fluoroquinolone. Risk of tendon rupture and peripheral neuropathy. Avoid in myasthenia gravis.
data.alert: Monitor renal and hepatic function during therapy.

data.fact: UTI-specific antibiotic. Turns urine brown. Avoid in renal impairment (CrCl <30).
data.alert: Watch for signs of C. difficile: watery diarrhea, fever.

data.fact: Omnicef is the trade name for Cefdinir. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Watch for signs of C. difficile: watery diarrhea, fever.

data.fact: Pen VK is the trade name for Penicillin V Potassium. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Complete the full course of therapy. Monitor for superinfection.

data.fact: Turns body fluids orange/red. Potent CYP450 inducer—many drug interactions. TB regimen essential drug.
data.alert: Assess for allergies before first dose. Monitor C&S results.

data.fact: 3rd-gen cephalosporin. Used for meningitis, gonorrhea, Lyme disease. Cross-reactivity with penicillin allergy (~1%).
data.alert: Report rash, hives, or difficulty breathing immediately.

data.fact: Avoid in pregnancy and children <8 (tooth discoloration). Take 1 hr before or 2 hrs after meals.
data.alert: Report rash, hives, or difficulty breathing immediately.

data.fact: Xifaxan is the trade name for Rifaximin. Drug class: Antibiotic.
data.alert: Assess for allergies before first dose. Monitor C&S results.

data.fact: Macrolide antibiotic. Take on empty stomach. Prolongs QT interval. Z-pack = 5-day course.
data.alert: Report rash, hives, or difficulty breathing immediately.

data.fact: MAO inhibitor properties. Avoid tyramine-rich foods. Monitor CBC weekly for thrombocytopenia.
data.alert: Monitor renal and hepatic function during therapy.

data.fact: Antiplatelet. Reversibly inhibits P2Y12. Do not use aspirin >100mg/day concurrently. Common side effect: dyspnea.
data.alert: Avoid IM injections. Fall prevention is critical.

data.fact: Vitamin K antagonist. Monitor INR (goal 2-3). Antidote: Vitamin K (phytonadione). Avoid cranberry/grapefruit.
data.alert: Avoid IM injections. Fall prevention is critical.

data.fact: Effient is the trade name for Prasugrel. Drug class: Anticoagulant / Antiplatelet.
data.alert: Check INR/PT for warfarin; aPTT for heparin.

data.fact: Direct oral anticoagulant (DOAC). No routine monitoring needed. Reversal agent: andexanet alfa.
data.alert: Check INR/PT for warfarin; aPTT for heparin.

data.fact: Low molecular weight heparin. SC injection in abdomen. Do not expel air bubble. No routine aPTT monitoring needed.
data.alert: Hold pressure on venipuncture sites for 5+ minutes.

data.fact: DOAC (Factor Xa inhibitor). Take with evening meal for absorption. Reversal: andexanet alfa.
data.alert: Avoid IM injections. Fall prevention is critical.

data.fact: TCA. First-line for OCD. Strong serotonergic activity. High anticholinergic effects.
data.alert: Monitor weight, sleep patterns, and mood changes.

data.fact: Celexa is the trade name for Citalopram. Drug class: Antidepressant.
data.alert: Monitor weight, sleep patterns, and mood changes.

data.fact: Often used off-label for insomnia. Risk of priapism. Take with food. Causes orthostatic hypotension.
data.alert: Takes 2-4 weeks for full therapeutic effect. Monitor for suicidality.

data.fact: SNRI. Monitor blood pressure (can increase). Taper slowly to avoid discontinuation syndrome.
data.alert: Do not stop abruptly—taper to avoid withdrawal symptoms.

data.fact: TCA. Anticholinergic effects. Overdose is life-threatening (cardiac toxicity). Used for neuropathic pain.
data.alert: Assess for serotonin syndrome: agitation, hyperthermia, tremor.

data.fact: SSRI. S-enantiomer of citalopram. First-line for anxiety and depression. QT prolongation at high doses.
data.alert: Takes 2-4 weeks for full therapeutic effect. Monitor for suicidality.

data.fact: Pamelor is the trade name for Nortriptyline. Drug class: Antidepressant.
data.alert: Do not stop abruptly—taper to avoid withdrawal symptoms.

data.fact: SSRI. Strong CYP2D6 inhibitor. Higher anticholinergic effects than other SSRIs. Avoid in pregnancy.
data.alert: Monitor weight, sleep patterns, and mood changes.

data.fact: SSRI. Long half-life. Avoid MAOIs. Monitor for serotonin syndrome. May increase suicidal ideation in youth.
data.alert: Do not stop abruptly—taper to avoid withdrawal symptoms.

data.fact: Increases appetite and causes sedation. Weight gain common. Useful in underweight depressed patients.
data.alert: Monitor weight, sleep patterns, and mood changes.

data.fact: Sinequan is the trade name for Doxepin. Drug class: Antidepressant.
data.alert: Avoid MAOIs and tyramine-rich foods with certain classes.

data.fact: TCA. Used for depression and enuresis in children. Cardiotoxic in overdose. Monitor ECG.
data.alert: Monitor weight, sleep patterns, and mood changes.

data.fact: Viibryd is the trade name for Vilazodone. Drug class: Antidepressant.
data.alert: Assess for serotonin syndrome: agitation, hyperthermia, tremor.

data.fact: NDRI antidepressant. Also used for smoking cessation. Lowers seizure threshold. No sexual side effects.
data.alert: Assess for serotonin syndrome: agitation, hyperthermia, tremor.

data.fact: SSRI. First-line for depression, OCD, PTSD. GI side effects common initially. Avoid abrupt discontinuation.
data.alert: Assess for serotonin syndrome: agitation, hyperthermia, tremor.

data.fact: Thiazolidinedione. Monitor liver function. Causes fluid retention. Contraindicated in heart failure.
data.alert: Teach patient to carry fast-acting glucose source.

data.fact: Sulfonylurea. Once daily. Risk of hypoglycemia. Take with breakfast. Avoid in sulfa allergy.
data.alert: Teach patient to carry fast-acting glucose source.

data.fact: SGLT2 inhibitor. Glucosuria, genital infections, DKA risk. Monitor renal function. Cardiovascular benefit.
data.alert: Hold metformin before contrast dye procedures.

data.fact: Sulfonylurea. Longer acting than glipizide. Higher hypoglycemia risk, especially in elderly.
data.alert: Monitor blood glucose regularly. Teach signs of hypo/hyperglycemia.

data.fact: SGLT2 inhibitor. Heart failure and CKD benefit. Risk of DKA and genital mycotic infections. Monitor glucose.
data.alert: Assess for hypoglycemia: tremors, sweating, confusion.

data.fact: First-line for Type 2 DM. Hold 48 hrs before/after contrast dye. Risk of lactic acidosis. Does not cause hypoglycemia alone.
data.alert: Assess for hypoglycemia: tremors, sweating, confusion.

data.fact: Sulfonylurea. Stimulates insulin release. Risk of hypoglycemia. Take 30 min before meals.
data.alert: Assess for hypoglycemia: tremors, sweating, confusion.

data.fact: Rapid-acting insulin. Onset 15 min. Give immediately before meals. Clear appearance.
data.alert: Monitor blood glucose regularly. Teach signs of hypo/hyperglycemia.

data.fact: Humulin is the trade name for Insulin Isophane / Insulin Regular. Drug class: Antidiabetic.
data.alert: Rotate insulin injection sites. Never shake insulin vials.

data.fact: SGLT2 inhibitor. Causes glucosuria. Risk of UTIs and genital infections. Cardiovascular benefit.
data.alert: Rotate insulin injection sites. Never shake insulin vials.

data.fact: Long-acting insulin. Do NOT mix with other insulins. Give at same time daily. Clear appearance.
data.alert: Assess for hypoglycemia: tremors, sweating, confusion.

data.fact: Regular/NPH human insulin. Regular: only insulin for IV use. NPH: intermediate-acting, cloudy, do not give IV.
data.alert: Rotate insulin injection sites. Never shake insulin vials.

data.fact: Rapid-acting insulin. Onset 10-20 min. Give 5-10 min before meals. Clear solution.
data.alert: Hold metformin before contrast dye procedures.

data.fact: DPP-4 inhibitor. No renal dosing needed. Low hypoglycemia risk. Joint pain possible.
data.alert: Hold metformin before contrast dye procedures.

data.fact: GLP-1 agonist. SC injection. Promotes weight loss. Black box: thyroid C-cell tumors in animals.
data.alert: Hold metformin before contrast dye procedures.

data.fact: Topical antifungal for skin and vaginal infections. OTC. Interacts with warfarin even topically.
data.alert: Monitor hepatic function tests. Many antifungals are hepatotoxic.

data.fact: Systemic antifungal. Monitor liver function. Many drug interactions via CYP450. Single dose treats vaginal candidiasis.
data.alert: Complete full course even if symptoms resolve early.

data.fact: Topical antifungal for skin and nail infections. Apply to affected area. Complete full course.
data.alert: Apply topical forms to clean, dry skin.

data.fact: Topical antifungal. OTC. Available as cream, lozenge, vaginal. Minimal systemic absorption.
data.alert: Apply topical forms to clean, dry skin.

data.fact: Topical antifungal for skin and vaginal infections. OTC. Interacts with warfarin even topically.
data.alert: Monitor hepatic function tests. Many antifungals are hepatotoxic.

data.fact: Systemic antifungal. Hepatotoxic. Strong CYP3A4 inhibitor. Monitor liver enzymes closely.
data.alert: Monitor hepatic function tests. Many antifungals are hepatotoxic.

data.fact: Topical/oral antifungal. Swish and swallow for oral thrush. Not absorbed systemically.
data.alert: Apply topical forms to clean, dry skin.

data.fact: Systemic antifungal. Take with food (capsules). Hepatotoxic. Negative inotropic—avoid in HF.
data.alert: Monitor hepatic function tests. Many antifungals are hepatotoxic.

data.fact: Topical antifungal. OTC. Used for prevention and treatment of tinea. Minimal side effects.
data.alert: Report rash, jaundice, or dark urine immediately.

data.fact: Xanthine oxidase inhibitor for gout. Alternative to allopurinol. Black box: increased cardiovascular death risk.
data.alert: Avoid alcohol and purine-rich foods during therapy.

data.fact: Xanthine oxidase inhibitor. Prevents gout attacks. Increase fluids. Take with meals to reduce GI upset.
data.alert: Report rash immediately; may indicate hypersensitivity.

data.fact: Antihistamine nasal spray. Bitter taste common. Used for allergic and vasomotor rhinitis.
data.alert: Assess for dry mouth; encourage oral hygiene.

data.fact: First-gen antihistamine. Strong sedative/anticholinergic effects. Used for allergies, insomnia, EPS.
data.alert: Causes drowsiness—avoid driving until response is known.

data.fact: Second-gen antihistamine. Non-sedating. OTC. Minimal anticholinergic effects.
data.alert: First-generation agents have stronger anticholinergic effects.

data.fact: Phenothiazine antihistamine. IV/IM causes tissue necrosis. Black box: no IV push in children <2.
data.alert: Causes drowsiness—avoid driving until response is known.

data.fact: Second-gen antihistamine. Less sedating but can cause drowsiness. OTC. Renal dosing needed.
data.alert: Avoid alcohol and other CNS depressants.

data.fact: Alkylating agent. Risk of hemorrhagic cystitis—push fluids + mesna. Monitor CBC.
data.alert: Handle with cytotoxic precautions. Wear PPE.

data.fact: Used in sickle cell disease and CML. Monitor CBC closely. Causes bone marrow suppression.
data.alert: Assess for tumor lysis syndrome: hyperkalemia, hyperuricemia.

data.fact: Tyrosine kinase inhibitor for CML. Pleural effusions, myelosuppression. Monitor CBC and chest imaging.
data.alert: Monitor CBC—watch for bone marrow suppression.

data.fact: Atypical antipsychotic. Partial dopamine agonist. Less metabolic effects. Akathisia is common side effect.
data.alert: Monitor metabolic panel—weight, glucose, lipids.

data.fact: Atypical antipsychotic. Requires weekly-to-monthly ANC monitoring for agranulocytosis. REMS program.
data.alert: Clozapine requires regular WBC monitoring for agranulocytosis.

data.fact: Atypical antipsychotic. Must take with food (400 kcal). QT prolongation risk. Lower metabolic effects.
data.alert: Clozapine requires regular WBC monitoring for agranulocytosis.

data.fact: Typical antipsychotic. High EPS risk. Monitor for QT prolongation. IM decanoate for long-acting.
data.alert: Monitor for extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS) and tardive dyskinesia.

data.fact: Active metabolite of risperidone. Extended-release. Available as monthly injection. Monitor prolactin.
data.alert: Watch for neuroleptic malignant syndrome: fever, rigidity, altered LOC.

data.fact: Typical antipsychotic. Inhaled form for acute agitation. Monitor for bronchospasm (REMS program).
data.alert: Clozapine requires regular WBC monitoring for agranulocytosis.

data.fact: Typical antipsychotic. High potency. EPS risk. Monitor for NMS. Photosensitivity.
data.alert: Assess orthostatic blood pressure. Rise slowly from sitting.

data.fact: Typical antipsychotic. High potency. Available as long-acting decanoate injection. High EPS risk.
data.alert: Monitor metabolic panel—weight, glucose, lipids.

data.fact: Atypical antipsychotic. Lower dose for elderly. Monitor prolactin levels. Less metabolic effects than olanzapine.
data.alert: Watch for neuroleptic malignant syndrome: fever, rigidity, altered LOC.

data.fact: Atypical antipsychotic. Sedating. Used off-label for insomnia. Monitor metabolic panel.
data.alert: Monitor metabolic panel—weight, glucose, lipids.

data.fact: Typical antipsychotic. Low potency. High sedation and anticholinergic effects. Photosensitivity.
data.alert: Clozapine requires regular WBC monitoring for agranulocytosis.

data.fact: Atypical antipsychotic. Long half-life. Used for schizophrenia and bipolar depression. Akathisia common.
data.alert: Monitor for extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS) and tardive dyskinesia.

data.fact: Atypical antipsychotic. Significant metabolic effects: weight gain, hyperglycemia, dyslipidemia.
data.alert: Monitor metabolic panel—weight, glucose, lipids.

data.fact: Antiseptic skin cleanser. Pre-surgical prep. Avoid eyes, ears, CNS. Allergic reactions possible.
data.alert: For external use only. Avoid contact with eyes and mucous membranes.

data.fact: Mild antiseptic. Used for vaginal infections (suppositories). Toxic if ingested. Not for broken skin.
data.alert: Chlorhexidine: avoid ear canal and CNS exposure.

data.fact: Dakin's solution. Wound antiseptic. Diluted bleach. Bactericidal. Irritating at higher concentrations.
data.alert: For external use only. Avoid contact with eyes and mucous membranes.

data.fact: Antiseptic skin cleanser. Pre-surgical prep. Avoid eyes, ears, CNS. Allergic reactions possible.
data.alert: For external use only. Avoid contact with eyes and mucous membranes.

data.fact: Biktarvy is the trade name for Bictegravir / Emtricitabine / Tenofovir Alafenamide. Drug class: Antiviral.
data.alert: Assess for signs of immune reconstitution syndrome with HIV drugs.

data.fact: Delstrigo is the trade name for Doravirine / Lamivudine / Tenofovir. Drug class: Antiviral.
data.alert: Start therapy as early as possible for maximum efficacy.

data.fact: Epclusa is the trade name for Sofosbuvir / Velpatasvir. Drug class: Antiviral.
data.alert: Assess for signs of immune reconstitution syndrome with HIV drugs.

data.fact: NRTI antiviral for HIV and hepatitis B. Minimal side effects. Resistance develops if used alone.
data.alert: Start therapy as early as possible for maximum efficacy.

data.fact: Genvoya is the trade name for Elvitegravir / Cobicistat / Emtricitabine / Tenofovir Alafenamide. Drug class: Antiviral.
data.alert: Assess for signs of immune reconstitution syndrome with HIV drugs.

data.fact: Mavyret is the trade name for Glecaprevir / Pibrentasvir. Drug class: Antiviral.
data.alert: Ensure strict adherence—missed doses can cause resistance.

data.fact: Neuraminidase inhibitor for influenza. Start within 48 hrs of symptoms. Nausea common.
data.alert: Ensure strict adherence—missed doses can cause resistance.

data.fact: Integrase inhibitor for HIV. Take with or without food. Few drug interactions. Well tolerated.
data.alert: Start therapy as early as possible for maximum efficacy.

data.fact: Triumeq is the trade name for Abacavir / Dolutegravir / Lamivudine. Drug class: Antiviral.
data.alert: Maintain adequate hydration to prevent crystalluria.

data.fact: Truvada is the trade name for Emtricitabine / Tenofovir Disoproxil Fumarate. Drug class: Antiviral.
data.alert: Assess for signs of immune reconstitution syndrome with HIV drugs.

data.fact: Prodrug of acyclovir. Better oral bioavailability. Used for herpes and shingles. Maintain hydration.
data.alert: Ensure strict adherence—missed doses can cause resistance.

data.fact: NRTI. Nephrotoxic—monitor renal function. Bone density loss. Available as two prodrug forms.
data.alert: Ensure strict adherence—missed doses can cause resistance.

data.fact: Antiviral for HSV and VZV. Maintain hydration to prevent crystalluria/nephrotoxicity. Dose adjust for renal impairment.
data.alert: Maintain adequate hydration to prevent crystalluria.

data.fact: Bisphosphonate. Take on empty stomach with full glass of water. Stay upright 30 min. Weekly dosing option.
data.alert: Assess for esophageal irritation: difficulty swallowing, chest pain.

data.fact: ACE inhibitor. Same class effects as lisinopril. Dry cough, hyperkalemia. Avoid in pregnancy.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Direct vasodilator. Reflex tachycardia common. Drug-induced lupus with prolonged use. Monitor ANA.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: ARB. GI side effects including sprue-like enteropathy (rare). Monitor renal function and potassium.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: Loop diuretic. More potent than furosemide (1:40 ratio). Same monitoring: K+, fluid status, hearing.
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: Bystolic is the trade name for Nebivolol. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Non-DHP calcium channel blocker. Constipation common. Monitor HR—avoid with beta-blockers. Grapefruit interaction.
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: Non-DHP calcium channel blocker. Monitor HR and BP. Used for rate control in AFib. Avoid in HF.
data.alert: Assess for orthostatic hypotension. Rise slowly.

data.fact: Alpha-1 blocker. Used for BPH and hypertension. First-dose syncope—give at bedtime initially.
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: Central alpha-2 agonist. Do not stop abruptly (rebound hypertension). Available as patch. Causes sedation.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Alpha/beta-blocker. Take with food. Monitor for hypotension and bradycardia. Used in heart failure.
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: Corgard is the trade name for Nadolol. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: ARB. Alternative to ACE inhibitors (no cough). Monitor potassium and renal function. Contraindicated in pregnancy.
data.alert: Assess for orthostatic hypotension. Rise slowly.

data.fact: Loop diuretic. Longer acting than furosemide. Monitor electrolytes. Less ototoxicity.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: Diovan is the trade name for Valsartan. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Entresto is the trade name for Sacubitril / Valsartan. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Imdur is the trade name for Isosorbide Mononitrate. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Non-selective beta-blocker. Bronchospasm risk—avoid in asthma. Masks hypoglycemia symptoms.
data.alert: Assess for orthostatic hypotension. Rise slowly.

data.fact: Selective aldosterone blocker. Like spironolactone but fewer sexual side effects. Monitor potassium.
data.alert: Assess for orthostatic hypotension. Rise slowly.

data.fact: Cardiac glycoside. Check apical pulse ×1 min before giving (hold if <60 bpm). Therapeutic range 0.5-2.0 ng/mL. Hypokalemia increases toxicity.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: Loop diuretic. Ototoxic at high doses. Monitor K+, Na+, fluid status. Give in AM to avoid nocturia.
data.alert: Assess for orthostatic hypotension. Rise slowly.

data.fact: Cardioselective beta-blocker. Hold if HR <60 or SBP <90. Do not stop abruptly. Monitor for bronchospasm.
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: Maxzide is the trade name for Triamterene / Hydrochlorothiazide. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: Thiazide diuretic. Monitor electrolytes (hypokalemia). Causes photosensitivity. Increases lithium levels.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Alpha-1 blocker. Used off-label for PTSD nightmares. First-dose hypotension—start at bedtime.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: Antiarrhythmic for AFib. Contraindicated in severe HF and permanent AFib. Monitor liver and lung function.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: Vasodilator. Sublingual for acute angina. Headache is expected. Hypotension: sit/lie down. No PDE5 inhibitors.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: Calcium channel blocker (DHP). Peripheral edema common. Monitor blood pressure. Grapefruit interaction.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Class III antiarrhythmic. Pulmonary toxicity, thyroid dysfunction, hepatotoxicity. Blue-gray skin discoloration. Monitor PFTs, TFTs, LFTs.
data.alert: Assess for orthostatic hypotension. Rise slowly.

data.fact: Antiplatelet/vasodilator for intermittent claudication. Contraindicated in heart failure. Take 30 min before meals.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: ACE inhibitor. Dry cough is common side effect. Monitor potassium (hyperkalemia). Contraindicated in pregnancy.
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: Alpha-1 agonist for orthostatic hypotension. Take early in day—do not give at bedtime (supine hypertension).
data.alert: Monitor electrolytes, especially potassium with diuretics.

data.fact: DHP calcium channel blocker. Reflex tachycardia. Peripheral edema. Do not use immediate-release sublingually.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Anti-anginal. Prolongs QT interval. Do not crush. Used as add-on therapy for chronic angina.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Sectral is the trade name for Acebutolol. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Class IC antiarrhythmic. Only use with structural heart disease exclusion. Proarrhythmic risk.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Tenex is the trade name for Guanfacine. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Cardioselective beta-blocker. Renally excreted. Hold for HR <60 or SBP <90. Less bronchospasm than non-selective.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Thalitone is the trade name for Chlorthalidone. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Check apical pulse for 1 full minute before giving digoxin.

data.fact: Alpha/beta-blocker. Used in hypertensive emergencies and pregnancy. IV: keep supine for 3 hrs after.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Zaroxolyn is the trade name for Metolazone. Drug class: Cardiovascular.
data.alert: Weigh daily for heart failure patients. Report gain >2 lbs/day.

data.fact: Cardioselective beta-blocker for HF. Titrate slowly. Monitor HR and BP. Do not stop abruptly.
data.alert: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate before administration.

data.fact: Cholinesterase inhibitor for Alzheimer's. GI upset common. Take at bedtime. Monitor for bradycardia.
data.alert: Do not stop anticonvulsants abruptly—risk of seizures.

data.fact: Anticholinergic for Parkinson's/EPS. Same side effects as benztropine. Avoid in elderly.
data.alert: Assess neurological status and level of consciousness.

data.fact: Benzodiazepine. Used for anxiety, seizures, alcohol withdrawal. Respiratory depression risk. Flumazenil reverses.
data.alert: Monitor for CNS depression: drowsiness, confusion, ataxia.

data.fact: Non-benzodiazepine anxiolytic. Takes 2-4 weeks for effect. No sedation/dependence. Cannot be used PRN.
data.alert: Therapeutic drug levels must be monitored for many CNS agents.

data.fact: Anticholinergic. Used for EPS from antipsychotics. Dry mouth, constipation, urinary retention, blurred vision.
data.alert: Assess neurological status and level of consciousness.

data.fact: Anticonvulsant/mood stabilizer. Hepatotoxic—monitor LFTs. Teratogenic. Monitor levels (50-100 mcg/mL).
data.alert: Do not stop anticonvulsants abruptly—risk of seizures.

data.fact: Anticonvulsant. Narrow therapeutic index (10-20 mcg/mL). Gingival hyperplasia. IV: cardiac monitoring required.
data.alert: Therapeutic drug levels must be monitored for many CNS agents.

data.fact: Muscle relaxant. Structurally similar to TCAs. Anticholinergic effects. Short-term use only (2-3 weeks).
data.alert: Therapeutic drug levels must be monitored for many CNS agents.

data.fact: Dopaminergic. Used for Parkinson's and drug-induced EPS. Livedo reticularis. Anticholinergic effects.
data.alert: Therapeutic drug levels must be monitored for many CNS agents.

data.fact: Anticonvulsant. Behavioral changes, irritability common. Renal dosing required. Fewer drug interactions.
data.alert: Monitor for CNS depression: drowsiness, confusion, ataxia.

data.fact: Benzodiazepine for seizures and panic disorder. Long-acting. Dependence risk. Taper to discontinue.
data.alert: Therapeutic drug levels must be monitored for many CNS agents.

data.fact: Anticonvulsant/mood stabilizer. Stevens-Johnson syndrome risk—titrate slowly. Rash = stop drug.
data.alert: Implement fall precautions. Monitor gait and coordination.

data.fact: Muscle relaxant. CNS depressant. Do not stop abruptly (seizures, hallucinations). Monitor for drowsiness.
data.alert: Do not stop anticonvulsants abruptly—risk of seizures.

data.fact: Magnesium supplement. Can cause diarrhea. Monitor levels in renal impairment. Antacid properties.
data.alert: Iron supplements: take on empty stomach with vitamin C.

data.fact: Sleep supplement. Take 30-60 min before bedtime. Relatively safe. May interact with anticoagulants.
data.alert: Monitor serum levels to prevent toxicity.

data.fact: Active form of Vitamin D. Monitor calcium levels. Hypercalcemia risk. Used in CKD and hypoparathyroidism.
data.alert: Calcium and iron should be taken 2 hours apart.

data.fact: Vitamin B1. Given before glucose in alcoholic patients to prevent Wernicke encephalopathy.
data.alert: Iron supplements: take on empty stomach with vitamin C.
